text: According to evaluation scholars, the purpose of the evaluation is to determine the success or the impact of a program and to understand its strengths and weaknesses which would in turn enable decision makers to enhance the program. This study examines the conceptual, objective and principle of Educational Program Evaluation (EPE) and the procedures that must be exercised during the evaluation process, the factors which promote and hinder the whole process of evaluation and how to strategically deal with the hindrances that hamper the evaluation process. Hence, this study based on systematic literature review makes an attempt to analyze and scrutinize the methodologies and approaches of the evaluations carried out in the developing world. Based on the scientific studies, the main purposes of the evaluation are to assess the effectiveness of a program, to determine its improvement needs and to improve educational quality. Therefore, in the context of education, an effective evaluation is the one that is based on objectivity and that is designed and directed to the target goals of a program. An educational program evaluation process usually involves three stages of planning, execution and evaluation. Moreover, several studies show that factors which promote the effectiveness of an evaluation include governmental and institutional policies ensuring adequacy in terms of budget and also the availability of qualified staff. Conversely, constraints which impede the whole evaluation process include lack of funding and insufficient number of staff.
